_description: Per-question rule tier and rationale for Norway vs England QF (2026-07-11). RULE1 restored this session: Smarkets fetched before any estimate was finalized, per the user's explicit decision after the Spain-Belgium postmortem. Full model-vs-market comparison in 03_model_derivations.json market_gap_log.

questions.text: Will Erling Haaland score a goal?
questions.rationale: 2026 empirical rate is 4/4 games played (unsustainable small-n), market 0.4368. Leaned above market given the validated Brazil-vs-Norway precedent for the same adjustment, but nowhere near the raw empirical rate -- RULE6 discipline against near-certain submissions applies regardless.

Gap under the 15pp recheck trigger (11.5pp). Market (0.4855) already sits between 2026 hot form (0.60) and 12-game historical caution (0.167) -- treated as a validation the market already priced this tension correctly. Small upward nudge for genuine recent volume increase.

31.9pp gap between 2026 empirical (0.60) and market (0.28125) triggered the new mandatory recheck. 2022 WC historical record (n=5): 0/5 games with 2+ SOT, ceiling was 1. Historical record and market agree against the recent 2-game streak -- shaded down sharply. This is the clearest single validation this session that the restored RULE1 discipline catches real mispricings before submission, not after.

questions.text: Will the match have 3+ total goals?
questions.rationale: 18.5pp gap. Model's total-goals lambda (2.254) is well below both teams' own 2026 scoring environments (England 3.2, Norway 4.2 goals/game) and below the market-implied lambda (2.999, back-solved). Blended heavily toward market + empirical scoring logs.

questions.text: Will the match be tied at halftime?
questions.rationale: Model (0.4421) and market (0.42195) agree closely -- no recheck needed, averaged.

15.5pp gap, same root cause as Q4 (model's total-goals lambda runs low). Norway scored AND conceded in literally every game this tournament (5/5). Blended heavily toward market + Norway's empirical BTTS record.

EB-shrunk Poisson (0.5162) and the exact-match Smarkets line (0.50685) agree closely -- no recheck needed. Regime-coverage check run explicitly (see 03_model_derivations.json) given this is one half of a paired count-prop with Q8; no asymmetric shading found necessary since this matchup is not a blowout-level mismatch.

No exact market line (Smarkets offers O/U4.5, not 3.5) -- rather than mark NA per last session's convention, calibrated an implied lambda from the nearby line via brentq and re-evaluated at the actual threshold (0.6010), then blended with the EB-shrunk Poisson model (0.6497). Regime-coverage check run explicitly, same as Q7 -- this is the direct fix for the exact failure mode that cost 0.85 on Belgium's SOT prop yesterday, applied here as standard practice rather than an afterthought.

questions.text: Will a substitute score or assist?
questions.rationale: New base rate built this session from 60 locally saved full ESPN match dumps (33/60 = 0.55). No market exists for this exact prop. Regressed slightly toward 0.5 given this is a corpus-wide rate, not match-specific.

questions.text: Will a penalty be awarded OR a red card shown?
questions.rationale: Component-wise model (0.3844) and market-derived OR combination (0.3836) agree almost exactly -- strong validation, no correction needed.

questions.text: Will at least one card be shown in each half?
questions.rationale: New base rate built this session (29/60 = 0.4833) via keyEvents card-timing parse. Turpin's own small sample (1/2 = 0.50, only 2 known matches) agrees closely. No exact market exists. Blended to 0.48.

No exact market line (Smarkets offers O/U25.5, not 23.5). Same calibration technique as Q8: backed out an implied combined-shots lambda (25.535) from the O/U25.5 price and re-evaluated at 24+ (0.6460), blended with the naive team-log Poisson model (0.7191).

questions.text: Will the second half produce more goals than the first half?
rules: mandatory-recheck (>15pp gap between sim and market)
questions.rationale: Monte Carlo sim (0.3745) uses a uniform(0,93) goal-time assumption that doesn't capture the real second-half scoring bias (fatigue, subs, game-state chasing). Market's equivalent 'Highest Scoring Half -> Second Half' contract (0.46515) and the 60-match corpus base rate (0.4333) agree with each other and disagree with the naive sim -- both trusted over the sim's uniform-time artifact.

questions.text: Will England have more corners AND more shots than Norway?
questions.rationale: No market exists for this exact compound question. Computed via Skellam distribution on each marginal, combined with a partial-correlation heuristic (50/50 blend of the independence product and the high-correlation minimum-bound), since both indicators plausibly but not perfectly co-move with territorial dominance.

Blended model (0.4700) and market Full-time-result (0.4975) agree closely (2.75pp gap) -- no recheck needed, averaged with a small lean toward market given RULE1's standing precedent that verified sharp markets are at least as reliable as the model on match-winner questions.
